comparisons.description: Centralized cloud-based platform to manage wired and wireless infrastructure.

extreme.summary: ExtremeCloud IQ (XIQ) — AI-driven SaaS with single pane of glass for switches and APs. Deep ML insights.

cisco.summary: Cisco DNA Center / Catalyst Center — powerful but complex, primarily on-prem with cloud option.

juniper.summary: Juniper Mist — AI-native cloud management with strong wireless focus and Marvis AI assistant.

aruba.summary: Aruba Central — unified cloud management for campus and branch with AI insights.

fortinet.summary: FortiManager + FortiCloud — primarily security-focused, limited network cloud management.

comparisons.description: AI/ML-driven network insights, predictive analytics, and automated remediation.

extreme.summary: XIQ uses ML for anomaly detection, capacity planning, and automated network optimization.

cisco.summary: ThousandEyes + Catalyst Center AI Analytics provide strong telemetry and intent-based automation.

juniper.summary: Marvis AI — industry-leading conversational AI assistant for network ops troubleshooting.

aruba.summary: Aruba AIOps with UEBA for user and entity behavior analytics across campus.

fortinet.summary: FortiAI focused on threat detection and security automation rather than network operations.

comparisons.description: Support for Wi-Fi 6E (802.11ax with 6 GHz band) access points.

extreme.summary: Full Wi-Fi 6E portfolio (AP460C, AP4000 series) with 6 GHz tri-band support.

cisco.summary: Catalyst 9136 and 9166 series — robust Wi-Fi 6E portfolio with Catalyst Center integration.

juniper.summary: AP45 and AP43 series with Wi-Fi 6E and Mist cloud management.

aruba.summary: AP-635 and AP-655 series — comprehensive Wi-Fi 6E portfolio with Aruba Central.

fortinet.summary: FortiAP 433G/443G — Wi-Fi 6E available but security-integration is primary differentiator.

comparisons.description: Automated campus or data center fabric technology for simplified L2/L3 operations.

extreme.summary: Fabric Engine (VOSS/SPB) — zero-touch IEEE 802.1aq fabric with loop-free L2/L3 in one step.

cisco.summary: Cisco SD-Access using LISP+VXLAN+ISE — powerful but complex to deploy and manage.

juniper.summary: Juniper Apstra — intent-based fabric automation with vendor-agnostic data center fabric.

aruba.summary: Aruba ESP (Edge Services Platform) with CX switches — automated fabric with dynamic segmentation.

fortinet.summary: FortiLink fabric — switching management through FortiGate, limited fabric automation.

comparisons.description: Automated IoT device discovery, classification, and policy enforcement.

extreme.summary: Extreme Defender for IoT — agentless, automatic device discovery and micro-segmentation.

cisco.summary: Cisco Cyber Vision + ISE — deep IoT visibility and policy for industrial and campus IoT.

juniper.summary: Juniper Mist provides IoT WLAN segmentation, limited wired IoT discovery.

aruba.summary: Aruba ClearPass — strong IoT profiling, dynamic role assignment, and BYOD/IoT security.

fortinet.summary: FortiNAC — dedicated NAC solution with IoT device classification and automated quarantine.

comparisons.description: REST APIs, NETCONF/YANG, and developer ecosystem support.

extreme.summary: XIQ Open API, EXOS Python scripting, NETCONF/YANG — strong open programmability stack.

cisco.summary: Cisco DevNet — largest networking developer community with extensive APIs and toolkits.

juniper.summary: Junos Automation — PyEZ, Ansible, NETCONF/YANG, strong open-source contributions.

aruba.summary: Aruba Central APIs + ArubaOS-CX scripting — good REST API ecosystem.

fortinet.summary: FortiOS REST API — primarily security-oriented, limited network automation APIs.

comparisons.description: Pricing transparency, subscription model, and total cost of ownership.

extreme.summary: Flexible subscription + perpetual options. XIQ has a free tier. Transparent, straightforward.

cisco.summary: Complex tiered licensing (DNA Advantage, Essentials, etc.) with high TCO for enterprise.

juniper.summary: Mist subscription — clear cloud licensing but hardware + cloud costs can add up.

aruba.summary: Aruba Central subscription — straightforward but HPE ecosystem lock-in.

fortinet.summary: Bundled security + networking licenses — good value for security-first organizations.

comparisons.description: Primary market vertical and deployment sweet spots.

extreme.summary: Strong in stadiums, education, healthcare, and enterprise campus. #1 in sports venues globally.

cisco.summary: Dominant across all verticals — enterprise, service provider, data center, and SMB.

juniper.summary: Strong in service provider, cloud data center, and increasingly enterprise campus.

aruba.summary: Strong in enterprise campus, retail, healthcare, and BYOD-heavy environments.

fortinet.summary: Security-first — strong in SMB, distributed enterprise, and SD-Branch deployments.
